messages.content:
[GT.03]Chemical evolution explains how living systems were created from

 living atoms
 animal atoms
 plant molecules
 non-living molecules
non-living molecules.

Australia is in the Southern hemisphere. Which of these statements is correct about Australia?

 It experiences the first day of summer at Position 3 and first day of winter at Position 1.
 It experiences the first day of summer at Position 1 and first day of winter at Position 3.
 It experiences the first day of autumn at Position 3 and first day of spring at Position 1.
 It experiences the first day of autumn at Position 1 and first day of spring at Position 3.
It experiences the first day of summer at Position 1 and first day of winter at Position 3.

[OSS.01]The laws surrounding the motion of planets in our solar system

 can be replaced by new laws
 describe what happens in nature under certain conditions
 prove that theories on space are non-testable
 can be used to prove that theories on gravity are non-observable
describe what happens in nature under certain conditions.

[OSS.03]Scientists have observed a huge loop of plasma shooting up from the sun's surface. Which of these events would most likely occur due to this solar condition?

 atmospheric pollution levels would increase
 atmospheric pollution levels would decrease
 atmospheric temperatures would decrease worldwide
 atmospheric temperatures would increase worldwide
Atmospheric temperatures would increase worldwide.

[O.05]A coastal location in Florida experiences very strong winds throughout the year. What would the coastal community in the location most likely do?

 ban tourists from visiting the beach
 migrate to inland areas to avoid tsunamis
 discourage recreational activities on the beach
 install wind turbines to produce renewable energy
Install wind turbines to produce renewable energy.

If the original mass of the radioactive material is 500 grams, what will be its mass after 6 billion years?

 3.4 grams
 7.8 grams
 15.6 grams
 31.2 grams
3.4 grams.

[OSS.01]A group of students designed an experiment with a thread and a rubber stopper. The steps of the experiment are listed below.

Tie a rubber stopper to one end of a 20 cm long thread.
Hold the other end of the thread and whirl it in a circle five times.
Allow the thread to wind up on the finger.
Observe that the rubber stopper moves faster as it comes nearer to the finger.
The experiment most likely demonstrates
 Kepler's first law of planetary motion
 Kepler's second law of planetary motion
 Newton's second law of motion
 Newton's third law of motion
Kepler's second law of planetary motion.

[GT.03]A group of scientists have proposed a new theory about biological evolution. The new theory would be reliable if

 other scientists prove the results non-testable
 other scientists repeat and match the results
 it is not supported by lengthy data and observation
 it is accepted as a universal law of nature
Other scientists repeat and match the results.

Which of these statements best compares Feature A and Feature B?

 Feature A is a guyot and Feature B is a seamount.
 Feature B is a guyot and Feature A is a seamount.
 Feature A is an abyssal plain and Feature B is an ocean trench.
 Feature B is an abyssal plain and Feature A is an ocean trench.
Feature A is a guyot and Feature B is a seamount.

[OSS.01]Models of the solar system are used to

 make theories on space permanent
 make objects in space non-observable
 explain difficult concepts about space
 scale up representations of objects in space
Explain difficult concepts about space.

[OSS.03]Which of these may be damaged by increase in solar activity?

 computer equipment in space
 microwave ovens on Earth
 mobile phone sets
 washing machines
Computer equipment in space.

[OSS.01]Copernicus and Kepler were astronomers who had proposed theories about the solar system. Copernicus believed that Earth and other planets circled a stationary sun. Kepler believed that the sun rotated like planets. Which of these statements best explains why Copernicus and Kepler made different observations about the solar system?

 They used outdated data to make inferences.
 They used incorrect methods of experimentation.
 They had different goals which influenced their inferences.
 They could not match their observations with their calculations.
They had different goals which influenced their inferences.

[OSS.01]Which of these statements is most likely correct about the Nebular theory of formation of planets?

 It will become a law if it is proved valid.
 It will not be replaced by other theories in the future.
 It was based on the study of non-observable and non-testable data.
 It was proposed after current evidences were investigated several times.
It will become a law if it is proved valid.

[OSS.01]The distance from Saturn to Earth is 10 AU. What is the distance, in kilometers, between Saturn and Earth?

 1 million kilometers
 1.5 million kilometers
 1 billion kilometers
 1.5 billion kilometers
1.5 billion kilometers.

[GT.02]The weak nuclear force can transform

 protons into neutrons
 electrons into isotopes
 electrons into neutrons
 protons into isotopes
Protons into neutrons.

[OSS.04H]The stars in the night sky appear to be fixed on a curved surface called the

 celestial sphere
 ecliptic plane
 circumpolar curve
 coordinate grid
Celestial sphere.

TG.05H]Which of these statements best explains why the running costs for geothermal power plants are very low?

 They use very low cost heat pumps.
 Fuels do not have to be purchased to generate power.
 Their equipments can be installed and maintained at low cost.
 Low cost equipments are used to clean their carbon emission.
Fuels do not have to be purchased to generate power.

[GT.02]Iodine-131 has a half-life of 8.10 days. In how many days will 50 grams of Iodine-131 decay to one-eighth of its original amount?

 24.3 days
 32.4 days
 40.5 days
 48.6 days
24.3 days.

[TG.04]The paragraph below describes the process by which a geologic feature is formed.

"This feature is formed when molten rocks under the ground break through Earth's crust. They cool down and pile up on the surface of Earth."

Which of these geologic features is formed by the process described in the paragraph?

 folded mountain
 fault block mountain
 plateau mountain
 volcanic mountain
Volcanic mountain.

Based on the table, which of these conclusions is most likely correct?
 The half-life of Material 1 and Material 2 are equal.
 The half-life of Material 2 is double the half life of Material 1.
 The half-life of Material 2 is 1,910 years more than the half-life of Material 1.
 The half-life of Material 1 is 1,910 years more than the half-life of Material 2.
The half-life of Material 2 is double the half life of Material 1.

[OSS.01]The first three steps in the formation of the sun are listed below.

A cloud of gas and dust called nebula spins slowly.
The nebula collapses due to condensation of gases.
The cloud spins faster as it collapses.
Which of these statements best describes the next step in the formation of the sun?
 The contracting nebula flattens into a disk with a bulge at the center.
 The gas particles in the nebula repel each other and move apart.
 The nuclear fission of the gas molecules releases large amount of energy.
 The sun is created at the outermost portion of the condensed nebula.
The contracting nebula flattens into a disk with a bulge at the center.

[OSS.03]Scientists have observed that the average sunspots have increased in the last 50 years. Which of these statements best describes an effect of this increase in sunspots?

 Earth's temperature has become cooler.
 Earth's temperature has not changed over time.
 Earth's temperature has become warmer.
 Earth's temperature has reduced by fifty times.
Earth's temperature has become warmer.

[GT.02]Which of these statements is most likely correct about a weak nuclear force?

 It binds electrons and protons.
 It binds protons and neutrons.
 It is an attractive force.
 It is a repulsive force.
It is an attractive force.

Based on observations, it can be inferred that

 Layer D is the oldest and Layer A is the youngest
 Layer C is the oldest and Layer D is the youngest
 Layer D is the oldest and Layer C is the youngest
 Layer A is the oldest and Layer D is the youngest
Layer C is the oldest and Layer D is the youngest.

[GT.02]A scientist can pose appropriate scientific questions about dating rocks if he is

 disciplined
 biased
 imaginative
 patient
Disciplined.

[OSS.01]New theories surrounding the formation of our solar system are proposed after

 current evidences are studied
 current data is proved non-testable
 old theories become facts in nature
 old theories become scientific laws
Current evidences are studied.

[O.02]One group of students did an experiment to study the movement of ocean water. The steps of the experiment are listed below.

Fill a rectangular baking glass dish with water.
Place a lighted lamp near the left edge of the dish.
Place a plastic bag with ice in the water near the right edge of the dish.
Put a few drops of ink in the water.
Observe the ink flow from the side where the lamp is placed towards the side where the ice bag is placed.
What does the students' experiment most likely demonstrate?
 Surface water sinks when heated.
 Surface water moves from colder to warmer regions.
 Surface water moves from warmer to colder regions.
 Surface water moves irrespective of water temperature.
Surface water moves from warmer to colder regions.

[TG.05]Which of these statements best compares P waves and S waves of an earthquake?

 Both S waves and P waves travel through oxygen gas.
 Both S waves and P waves travel through carbon dioxide gas.
 S waves do not tavel through molten rocks but P waves travel through molten rocks.
 P waves do not tavel through molten rocks but S waves travel through molten rocks.
P waves do not travel through molten rocks but S waves travel through molten rocks.

[OSS.02]Which of these events is most likely to occur due to the interaction between Earth's magnetic field and charged particles blowing away from the sun?

 gravity in space decreases
 auroras are visible at the poles
 the magnetosphere becomes stronger
 microgravity is experienced by astronauts
Auroras are visible at the poles.

[TG.02]Which of these statements best describes a similarity between photosynthesis and dissolving in the sea?

 Both store carbon in the ground.
 Both take up carbon from the ground.
 Both release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.
 Both absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
Both absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

[O.01]Which of these is the location where sea floor spreading occurs?

 abyss
 guyot
 ocean trenches
 mid-ocean ridges
Mid-ocean ridges.

[TG.01]Soil erosion due to rainfall is an example of interactions between the geosphere and

 cryosphere
 hydrosphere
 atmosphere
 biosphere
Hydrosphere.
In your own words, explain the processes responsible for the formation of a reverse fault. simply in 3-5 sentences

A reverse fault is formed due to the compression of Earth's crustal plates caused by internal tectonic activity. The compression causes one side of the fault to buckle and rise, while the other side is forced to move down. The hanging wall moves up and over the footwall, thereby reversing the direction of the displacement.
In your own words, explain why the landscape of Florida is flat and does not consist of geologic features like mountains and canyons.simply in 3-5 sentences

Florida is a flat landscape because it was created as a result of sedimentation and deposition of materials brought in by the sea. The lack of significant tectonic activity and the absence of significant volcanic activity have resulted in a stable geologic environment, which has allowed for the accumulation of sediments over millions of years. This has given rise to the mostly low-lying landscape of Florida.

[OSS.03] In your own words, explain how the number of sunspots can affect the overall global climate on Earth.


in 3-5 sentences

The number of sunspots can affect the overall global climate on Earth because sunspots are connected with solar activity, which impacts the amount of energy emitted by the Sun. As the number of sunspots increases, the amount of energy radiating from the Sun increases too, which can warm the Earth and lead to climatic changes, such as temperature variations. Conversely, as the number of sunspots decreases, the amount of energy reaching the Earth decreases, which can cause the Earth to cool and result in changes in weather patterns. Thus, the number of sunspots can have an effect on overall global climate patterns.

[TG.05H] A specific location in America has closed down several fossil fuel units and set up geothermal plants. In your own words, explain how this would affect the environment of the location.

in 3-5 sentences

Closing down fossil fuel units and setting up geothermal plants in a specific location can help in reducing the level of pollution which is caused by the burning of fossil fuels. This will improve air quality in the region and reduce the release of greenhouse gases, which contribute to climate change. Additionally, geothermal plants generate clean and renewable energy while having a lower impact on the environment in terms of land use and water usage, which can have a positive impact on the ecosystem as well.
